seoTitle: How to Integrate Asana With Jira
metaDescription: Connect Asana and Jira so tasks and updates stay in sync between teams that use different project management tools.
intro: It's common for one team to run on Jira while another prefers Asana, and integrating the two prevents information from getting stuck in one system. This guide covers setting up a connection between Asana and Jira.

steps.heading: Pick an integration method
steps.body: Decide whether you'll use a marketplace connector app or a middleware tool like Zapier to link the two platforms.

steps.heading: Install the integration app
steps.body: Add the chosen integration from the Atlassian Marketplace or your automation platform of choice.

steps.heading: Authorize both accounts
steps.body: Sign in and grant access to both your Jira and Asana accounts so the integration can read and write data.

steps.heading: Set up the sync rules
steps.body: Define which fields map between the two tools, such as task name, status, and assignee.

steps.heading: Test with a sample task
steps.body: Create a test task in one tool and confirm it appears correctly in the other before rolling it out to your team.

faq.question: Is there a native integration between Asana and Jira?
faq.answer: There's no single official Atlassian-built integration; most teams rely on marketplace apps or automation tools like Zapier.

faq.question: Does syncing tasks between Asana and Jira cost money?
faq.answer: It depends on the integration tool chosen, since some marketplace apps and automation platforms require a paid plan.

faq.question: Can updates sync in both directions?
faq.answer: Many integration tools support two-way sync, though some setups only push data one direction depending on configuration.

faq.question: Will subtasks and comments sync too?
faq.answer: This depends on the specific integration's feature set; not all tools sync every field, including comments and subtasks.
